action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 12 trained 1 epoch in ~187 sec (batch_size=128) with val_loss=0.142. The best eval score remains 1018.63 from iteration 5 (ResNet hidden=256, 12 blocks, 5 epochs, no residual). The original best checkpoint was overwritten, so we must retrain. Analysis: With ~50 minutes left, we can safely train the proven architecture for 15 epochs. At ~3 min/epoch, this is ~45 min total. We should keep the exact architecture that scored 1018 (no residual, no channel weighting) but add light regularization (weight_decay=1e-5) and early stopping (patience=5) to squeeze out generalization. AMP will keep epoch times low. Predict.py should also be hardened to strip _orig_mod. prefixes in case torch.compile is ever used. Decision: Write a clean train.py for 15 epochs with early stopping, batch_size=128, hidden=256, num_blocks=12, dropout=0.0, residual=False. Run it now. Submit eval in the next iteration.
